reasoning: The resolution criteria require only a single day between the open date and June 1, 2025, where the words "tariff" or "tariffs" do not appear on the print front pages of *both* The New York Times and the Wall Street Journal. While tariffs under the Trump administration are a major, heavily covered news topic, the nature of print journalism—including varied news cycles, weekends featuring softer news or longer features, and competing major global/domestic events—makes it virtually certain that there will be at least one day when neither paper includes those specific words on its front page. Searches show no evidence of a permanent daily "tariff tracker" on the front page of either publication, making the streak entirely dependent on organic news coverage. Over a span of several months, the probability of the word organically appearing in at least one of the two papers every single day without a single gap is miniscule. Therefore, finding one day of omission for a "Yes" resolution is extremely likely.
